events.description: President Bush offers a program of special assistance for Poland, whose Communist government has agreed to negotiations with the opposition Solidarity party which produce a plan for free elections. Elections are held in August, 1989, which lead to the end of single-party rule in Poland.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush Offers Poland Assistance

events.title: Bush Condemns China’s Actions
events.description: In the wake of the Tiananmen Square massacres, President Bush announces a number of condemnatory actions, including the suspension of the sale of American weapons to China.
events.impact: Mass killing of people

events.title: Bail-Out Plan Revised
events.description: President Bush signs into law the Financial Institutions Reform, Recovery, and Enforcement Act of 1989, a compromise with Congress on the bail-out of savings and loans. This law differs from Bush's February 6 proposal of financing the bail-out from the Treasury Department through the sale of bonds. It offers $166 billion worth of aid to troubled savings and loans institutions and creates a new government body, the Resolution Trust Company, to oversee the merger or liquidation of troubled banks.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Bail-Out Plan Revised

events.title: Fair Labor Standards Amendments
events.description: President Bush signs the Fair Labor Standards Amendments of 1989, which by April 1991 would raise the minimum wage to $4.25 an hour. The law was a significant victory for Bush over congressional Democrats, who in the spring of 1989 passed a bill, which President Bush vetoed on June 13, that raised the minimum wage to $4.55.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Fair Labor Standards Amendments

events.title: New Anti-Drug Law
events.description: President Bush signs a new anti-drug law that provides more than $3 billion for expanded anti-drug programs, including treatment facilities, federal prison expansion, education, and law enforcement.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: New Anti-Drug Law

events.title: Bush Meets with Gorbachev
events.description: President Bush and Soviet President Mikhail Gorbachev hold their first meeting of Bush's presidency in the harbor of Valetta, Malta, to discuss nuclear disarmament and the strengthening of Soviet-American trade relations. Both leaders announce that the Cold War is effectively over. Gorbachev and the USSR
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush Meets with Gorbachev

events.title: Americans with Disabilities Act
events.description: President Bush signs the Americans with Disabilities Act, which affects over 43 million Americans and forbids discrimination in employment, public accommodations, and transportation.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Americans with Disabilities Act

events.title: Civil Rights Act Vetoed
events.description: President Bush vetoes the Civil Rights Act of 1990, stating that the bill would “introduce the destructive force of quotas into our nation's employment system.”
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Civil Rights Act Vetoed

events.title: Budget Law Signed
events.description: President Bush signs a budget law intended to reduce the federal budget by almost $500 billion over the next five years. The law includes $140 billion dollars in new taxes.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Budget Law Signed

events.title: Clean Air Act Signed
events.description: President Bush signs the Clean Air Act of 1990, which tightens air pollution standards and seeks to reduce urban smog, cut acid rain pollution by one-half, and eliminate industrial emissions of toxic chemicals by the end of the 20th century.
events.impact: Protected public health from air pollution

events.title: Immigration Act of 1990
events.description: President Bush signs the Immigration Act of 1990, the most extensive revision to immigration law in more than a half century. The new law allows for the admission of 700,000 aliens each year.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Immigration Act of 1990

events.title: Persian Gulf War Begins
events.description: The Persian Gulf War, code-named Operation Desert Storm, begins with a massive, American-led air attack on Iraq. Early on the morning of January 17, 1991, coalition forces led by the United States launched air strikes against Iraq. These strikes signaled the beginning of the military phase of the Persian Gulf War. In August 1990, Iraq invaded Kuwait, a neighboring country. Iraq's leader, Saddam Hussein, had long argued with Kuwait over the rights to certain oil-rich lands. He had been preparing to invade Kuwait for several weeks, but when the attack came-on August 2, 1990-it nevertheless surprised much of the world. Several countries in the Middle East had assured the United States that Hussein was massing his troops only to bluff Kuwait into meeting his demands. When the Iraqi leader sent 140,000 troops storming toward-and quickly taking-the Kuwaiti capital, President George H.W. Bush responded. The United States quickly intervened for a number of reasons. [truncated]
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Persian Gulf War Begins

events.title: South African Sanctions Removed
events.description: President Bush lifts most American sanctions against the Republic of South Africa, saying that the movement to end apartheid is now “irreversible.”
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: South African Sanctions Removed

events.title: 1991 Civil Rights Act
events.description: President Bush signs the Civil Rights Act of 1991, making it easier for employees to sue employers on grounds of discrimination.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: 1991 Civil Rights Act

events.title: Bush Meets With Yeltsin
events.description: At the presidential retreat at Camp David, Maryland, President Bush and Russian president Boris Yeltsin meet to discuss U.S.-Russian relations and officially declare the end of the Cold War.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush Meets With Yeltsin

events.title: Bush Wins Primary
events.description: President Bush wins the New Hampshire primary but faces a strong challenge from conservative media personality Patrick Buchanan. The conservative wing of the Republican Party supports Buchanan, revealing a division within the party.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush Wins Primary

events.title: Soviet Union Aid Proposed
events.description: President Bush announces an aid plan of $24 billion to spur democratic and a free market reforms in the former Soviet Union.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Soviet Union Aid Proposed

events.title: Bush Attends Earth Summit
events.description: Speaking at the Earth Summit in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, President Bush announces that the United States will not sign a treaty designed to protect rare and endangered animals and plants, saying that it would retard the development of technology and the protection of ideas. The United States does sign the Framework Convention on Climate Change aimed at preventing further global warming.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush Attends Earth Summit

events.title: Supplemental Appropriations Act Signed
events.description: President Bush signs a supplemental appropriations act that provides aid to inner cities, specifically Los Angeles, which is trying to recover from the Rodney King riots of April 1992.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Supplemental Appropriations Act Signed

events.title: Unemployment Compensation Amendments
events.description: President Bush signs the Unemployment Compensation Amendments of 1992, extending coverage to the unemployed for 26 weeks, following their initial 26 weeks of benefits. The previous day, the Labor Department announced that the unemployment rate had reached 7.8 percent, its highest level since 1984.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Unemployment Compensation Amendments

events.title: Clinton inaugurated
events.description: William Jefferson “Bill” Clinton is inaugurated as the forty-second President of the United States.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Clinton inaugurated

events.title: HRC to head Health Care Reform
events.description: President Clinton announces that First Lady Hillary Rodham Clinton will head the Task Force on National Health Care Reform. The President hopes to reform the nation's health care system so that all Americans have health insurance, ensuring what is called “universal coverage,” and to control the sky-rocketing costs of health care.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: HRC to head Health Care Reform

events.title: Family Medical Leave Act
events.description: President Clinton signs the Family Medical Leave Act that requires companies to provide workers with up to three months of unpaid leave for family and medical emergencies.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Family Medical Leave Act

events.title: Gore heads National Performance Review
events.description: President Clinton appoints Vice President Al Gore to head the National Performance Review, which will devise an initiative entitled “Reinventing Government.” The initiative streamlines government by reducing the number of federal employees; it also cuts federal spending as a percentage of GDP to levels unseen since the Kennedy administration.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Gore heads National Performance Review

events.title: Navy attacks Baghdad
events.description: Navy, under President Clinton's orders, attacks Iraqi intelligence operations in downtown Baghdad after learning that Iraqis had plotted to kill former President Bush during his April 1993 visit to Kuwait. The twenty-three tomahawk missiles fired reportedly kill eight people.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Navy attacks Baghdad

events.title: “Don’t Ask, Don’t Tell”
events.description: President Clinton announces an “honorable compromise” in the debate surrounding the participation of gay service members in the military. It was determined that these individuals would be allowed to serve, but could face military investigations if they acknowledged their orientation, as well as be expelled for it. The policy is labeled “Don't Ask, Don't Tell.”
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: “Don’t Ask, Don’t Tell”

events.title: Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act
events.description: President Clinton signs the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act. The legislation, which passes both houses of Congress by slim majorities, lays out a plan to reduce the budget deficit by $496 billion through 1998, using a combination of spending cuts and tax increases.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act

events.title: Clinton unveils healthcare plan
events.description: President Clinton unveils a plan for universal health care that would fix what he called a “badly broken” system. Clinton emphasizes that under his plan, all Americans would have high quality health care and would be able to choose their physicians.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Clinton unveils healthcare plan

events.title: American forces ambushed in Somalia
events.description: An elite American special forces unit searching for Somali warlord Mohammed Farah Aidid in Somalia's capital city of Mogadishu is ambushed by Aidid's forces, leaving eighteen Americans dead. Three days later, President Clinton announces that all American military personnel in Somalia will be home by March 31, 1994.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: American forces ambushed in Somalia

events.title: Brady Act
events.description: President Clinton signs the Brady Act, which requires a potential handgun purchaser to wait five days while a background check is performed by law enforcement officers.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Brady Act

events.title: NAFTA creates free trade zone
events.description: After a hard-fought battle in Congress, President Clinton signs the North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA), eliminating nearly every trade barrier between the United States, Canada, and Mexico, creating the world's largest free trade zone. Clinton Signs NAFTA On December 8, 1993, President Bill Clinton signed the North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA), which eliminated nearly every trade barrier between the United States, Canada, and Mexico, creating the world's largest free trade zone. The House of Representatives approved NAFTA on November 17, 1993, by a vote of 234 to 200. Remarkably the agreement's supporters included 132 Republicans and only 102 Democrats. That unusual combination reflected the challenges President Clinton faced in convincing Congress that the controversial piece of legislation would truly benefit all Americans. President George H.W. Bush was NAFTA's original sponsor, signing the deal on December 17, 1992. [truncated]
events.impact: Protected rights of people with disabilities

events.title: Clinton talks welfare reform
events.description: President Clinton unveils his welfare reform initiatives. Clinton had campaigned in 1992 on the issue, promising to “end welfare as we know it.”
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Clinton talks welfare reform

events.title: Israel-Jordan talks
events.description: President Clinton meets with Prime Minister Yitzhak Rabin of Israel and King Hussein of Jordan. The talks result in Israel and Jordan agreeing in principle to end nearly fifty years of official antagonism.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Israel-Jordan talks

events.title: Congress delays heath care reform
events.description: The White House and congressional leaders, including Senate Majority Leader George Mitchell (D-ME), announce that Clinton's ambitious plan for health care reform will not be acted upon in 1994. Clinton's initiatives fail to find support in Congress.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Congress delays heath care reform

events.title: Clinton expands law enforcement, death penalty
events.description: President Clinton signs into law the Violent Crime Control and Law Enforcement Act that includes provisions providing for the hiring of 100,000 more police and the expansion of the death penalty to cover more than 50 federal crimes. Title IV of this law is the Violence Against Women Act, which targets domestic violence, sexual violence, dating violence, and stalking. It works to prevent and respond to gender-based violence.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Clinton expands law enforcement, death penalty

events.title: Congressional Accountability Act
events.description: President Clinton signs the Congressional Accountability Act, requiring Congress to abide by the same anti-discrimination workplace rules that apply throughout the rest of the country.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Congressional Accountability Act

events.title: Emergency loans to Mexico
events.description: President Clinton authorizes the U.S. Treasury Department to make an emergency loan of up to $20 billion to Mexico to forestall a financial crisis threatening the interconnected Mexican and American economies.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Emergency loans to Mexico

events.title: Clinton urges peace in Ireland
events.description: During a tour of Europe, President Clinton urges the continuation of peace efforts in Northern Ireland where longstanding conflict between Irish Protestants and Catholics escalated to violence over issues of economic and political autonomy.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Clinton urges peace in Ireland

events.title: Clinton delivers State of the Union
events.description: President Clinton, in the annual State of the Union address, declares that “the era of big government is over.” More important, he positions himself as a centrist, moderate Democrat for the upcoming presidential election, hoping that these types of pronouncements will blunt Republican charges that he is too liberal.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Clinton delivers State of the Union

events.title: Line-item veto approved
events.description: President Clinton signs a bill giving him the power of the “line-item veto,” which had been requested by Presidents Reagan and Bush. With this new power, Clinton can veto specific items in spending and tax bills without vetoing the entire measure.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Line-item veto approved

events.title: “Safe, legal and rare”
events.description: President Clinton vetoes a bill that would have outlawed certain types of late-term abortions, namely the partial birth abortion. Clinton emerges during his presidency as a strong advocate of the “right to choose,” often stating his wish that abortions in the United States become “safe, legal, and rare.”
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: “Safe, legal and rare”

events.title: Troops to remain in Bosnia
events.description: President Clinton announces that American troops will likely remain in Bosnia as the major component of an international peacekeeping force for an additional eighteen months.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Troops to remain in Bosnia

events.title: Expanding health care coverage
events.description: President Clinton signs a health care reform bill that he expects to expand coverage for many Americans. The measure specifically allows workers who change or lose their jobs to keep their health insurance coverage.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Expanding health care coverage

events.title: Welfare restructured
events.description: President Clinton signs a welfare reform bill that radically restructures the American welfare system. The provisions of the new law limit recipients of welfare benefits and enact a “welfare to work” initiative. Clinton Signs “Welfare to Work” Bill On August 22, 1996, President Bill Clinton signed into law the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act, reflecting his campaign promise to “end welfare as we know it.” The act contained several provisions expressing the necessity of work, the primacy of states, and, ultimately, limited government provision. No longer entitled to cash assistance, families could only receive federal aid for a total of five years. States now would receive fixed block grants each year with substantial discretion over how to distribute them. The act also made many legal immigrants ineligible to receive public benefits and reduced spending on the Food Stamp Program and disability benefits for children. [truncated]
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Welfare restructured

events.title: Missile strikes Iraq
events.description: President Clinton orders a cruise missile strike against Iraq after Saddam Hussein leads a siege against the Kurdish city of Irbil in northern Iraq.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Missile strikes Iraq

events.title: Clinton reelected
events.description: President Clinton, with 49 percent of the vote, defeats Senator Bob Dole (R-KS), with 41 percent of the vote, for the presidency. Clinton becomes the first Democratic President since Franklin Roosevelt to win reelection to a second term.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Clinton reelected

events.title: Madeline Albright becomes Secretary of State
events.description: President Clinton selects Madeline Albright, the American ambassador to the United Nations, to serve as his Secretary of State. After winning Senate confirmation, Albright is sworn in on January 23, 1997, becoming the first women to hold the position.
events.impact: Created framework for international cooperation

events.title: Balanced budget signed
events.description: President Clinton signs legislation providing for a balanced budget by 2002, ending years of partisan wrangling between Clinton and Republican leaders.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Balanced budget signed

events.title: Zemin makes state visit
events.description: President Clinton welcomes President Jiang Zemin of China for a state visit.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Zemin makes state visit

events.title: Stabilizing Southeast Asia
events.description: President Clinton orders the United States government to contribute $3 billion to an international bail-out of Indonesia totaling over $22 billion. The Clinton administration argues that the bailout will help stabilize the shaky financial situation in Southeast Asia.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Stabilizing Southeast Asia

events.title: Wye River Memorandum
events.description: After nine days of negotiations in rural Maryland, Israeli leader Benjamin Netanyahu and Palestinian leader Yasser Arafat sign the Wye River Memorandum. President Clinton mediates the negotiations, which result in an agreement highlighted by a three-stage withdrawal of Israeli troops from the West Bank.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Wye River Memorandum

events.title: Retaliatory attacks on Iraq
events.description: President Clinton orders a three-day bombing attack against Iraq after Saddam Hussein refuses to cooperate with United Nations weapons inspectors.
events.impact: Created framework for international cooperation

events.title: Clinton delivers State of the Union
events.description: President Clinton delivers his State of the Union address to a joint session of Congress under remarkable circumstances: six days earlier, the Senate had convened an impeachment trial against the President. Despite the impeachment process, public opinion polls show Clinton with his highest approval ratings.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Clinton delivers State of the Union

events.title: Permanent trade relations with China
events.description: President Clinton sends a bill to Congress asking for permanent normal trade relations with China. After securing House (May 24) and Senate (September 19) approval, Clinton signs the bill on October 10.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Permanent trade relations with China

events.title: Israeli peace summit
events.description: President Clinton hosts Israeli leader Ehud Barak and Palestinian leader Yasser Arafat at Camp David in the hope of reaching a peace agreement. After two weeks of unsuccessful talks, the summit breaks up with no agreement.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Israeli peace summit

events.title: George W. Bush inaugurated
events.description: Texas governor George W. Bush is inaugurated as the forty-third President of the United States.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: George W. Bush inaugurated

events.title: George W. Bush inaugurated
events.description: Bush is inaugurated as the 43rd President of the United States. He is the second son of a President to occupy the Oval Office, the first being John Quincy Adams in 1825.
events.impact: Protected rights of people with disabilities

events.title: Bush bans abortion aid
events.description: In one of his first policy decisions, President Bush decides to reinstate the ban on aid to international groups performing or counseling on abortion. The ban was initiated by former President Ronald Reagan but is not enforced during the administration of President Bill Clinton.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush bans abortion aid

events.title: Deregulating religious charities
events.description: By executive order, President Bush creates the Office of Faith-Based and Community Initiatives. The new office will work to ease regulations on religious charities and promote grass-roots efforts to tackle community issues such as aid to the poor and disadvantaged.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Deregulating religious charities

events.title: Attacking Iraq
events.description: United States airplanes attack Iraqi radar sites to enforce a “no-fly zone.” Bush calls the military action a “routine mission.”
events.impact: Protected rights of people with disabilities

events.title: Bush backs Taiwan over China
events.description: President Bush signals a change in relations with China by officially pledging military support for Taiwan in the event of an attack by China. This is the first time a presidential administration has publicly acknowledged a position that had previously been implicitly accepted.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush backs Taiwan over China

events.title: Trillion dollar tax cut
events.description: President Bush signs a $1.35 trillion tax cut into law. Although the amount falls short of the $1.60 trillion the administration has been seeking, the bill does slash income tax rates across the board and provides for the gradual elimination of the estate tax.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trillion dollar tax cut

events.title: Halting stem cell research
events.description: President Bush addresses the nation, outlining his plans for the federal funding of stem cell research. The new policy allows for continued government funding of existing research on already extracted stem cells but prohibits the extraction of additional stem cells from human embryos for further research.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Halting stem cell research

events.title: Operation “Enduring Freedom”
events.description: Speaking from the Treaty Room of the White House, President Bush announces the commencement of military action in Afghanistan, an operation code-named “Enduring Freedom.” Operation Enduring Freedom Begins On October 7, 2001, President George W. Bush announced that the United States had begun military action in Afghanistan. The military operation was code-named Enduring Freedom. “On my order, U.S. forces have begun strikes on terrorist camps of Al Qaeda and the military installations of the Taliban regime in Afghanistan,” Bush said in a somber, televised address from the White House Treaty Room. The air assaults, he said, were joined by Britain, with assorted intelligence efforts and logistical support coming from several other nations, including France, Germany, Australia, and Canada. [truncated]
events.impact: Protected rights of people with disabilities

events.title: D.C. Anthrax scare
events.description: The Capital shuts down amidst an Anthrax scare. Persons in Florida and New York have already tested positive for the frequently fatal bacteria. Bush calls for $1.5 billion to fight bioterrorism.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: D.C. Anthrax scare

events.title: No Child Left Behind
events.description: President Bush signs an education reform bill with bipartisan support; this reauthorized the Johnson-era Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965. Known as the No Child Left Behind Act, it offers local authorities greater flexibility in spending federal dollars, but requires standardized math and reading tests.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: No Child Left Behind

events.title: Bush delivers State of the Union
events.description: In his State of the Union address, President Bush warns that the war against terrorism is only beginning. Specifically citing North Korea, Iran, and Iraq, Bush speaks of “an axis of evil” threatening world peace.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush delivers State of the Union

events.title: Jimmy Carter travels to Cuba, visits Castro
events.description: Former President Jimmy Carter travels to Cuba for a tour and visit with Cuban President Fidel Castro. His arrival marks the first trip by an American President in forty years. On May 20, President Bush announces that the forty-year-old trade embargo against Cuba will continue until conditions, including free and fair elections, are met.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Jimmy Carter travels to Cuba, visits Castro

events.title: Bush expands Homeland Security
events.description: In a televised address to the nation , President Bush announces broad changes to security departments in charge of protecting the nation from terrorism. The Office of Homeland Security will now coordinate a wide range of functions and oversee more than 100 organizations. The announcement follows criticism of the FBI and CIA for failing to prevent the September 11 attacks.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush expands Homeland Security

events.title: Bush calls for new Palestinian leadership
events.description: President Bush calls for the Palestinian people to replace Yasser Arafat, head of the Palestinian Authority and leader of the Palestinian cause for more than thirty years. Bush states that “when the Palestinians have new leaders, institutions and security arrangements, the U.S. will support the creation of a Palestinian state.”
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush calls for new Palestinian leadership

events.title: Curbing corporate abuses
events.description: Following the Enron and WorldCom scandals, in which both companies claimed profits which turned out to be highly inflated, President Bush calls for new laws on corporate abuse. On July 10, the Dow Jones index drops below 9,000, its largest one-day loss since September 2001.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Curbing corporate abuses

events.title: Action against Iraq
events.description: Seeking support for action against Iraq, President Bush addresses Congress, identifying Iraqi strongman Saddam Hussein as “a serious threat.” Bush mentions the concept of a regime change and announces the visit of British Prime Minister Tony Blair in the days to come. House Majority Whip Tom DeLay (R-TX) calls action in Iraq “inevitable.”
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Action against Iraq

events.title: Bush addresses UN Security Council
events.description: President Bush addresses the United Nations’ Security Council, making his case for military action to enforce UN resolutions in Iraq. Additionally, he warns that the United States will move alone if the Council does not act. In the coming days, Bush and Secretary of State Colin Powell will continue to press the Security Council for a resolution against Iraq. France, Germany, and Russia, all permanent members of the Security Council, express severe reservations.
events.impact: Created framework for international cooperation

events.title: Taking military action in Iraq
events.description: After months of debate in the United Nations Security Council, President Bush announces the U.S. intention to move against Iraq with its coalition of allies. Bush issues an ultimatum for military action, giving Iraqi leader Saddam Hussein and his sons forty-eight hours to leave Iraq.
events.impact: Created framework for international cooperation

events.title: Bush declares war with Iraq
events.description: The 8:00 p.m. deadline for Hussein to leave Iraq passes. At 10:15 p.m., Bush addresses the nation and informs the American people that the United States is at war with Iraq.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush declares war with Iraq

events.title: Bush signs third largest tax cut in history
events.description: Bush signs into law his $350 billion tax-cut package, the third-largest in history, in an effort to strengthen the U.S. economy and reverse a trend of increasing unemployment. Congressional Democrats who opposed the bill argued it is skewed towards the wealthy.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Bush signs third largest tax cut in history

events.title: Bush, Sharon, Abbas meet
events.description: Bush meets with Israeli Prime Minister Ariel Sharon and Palestinian Prime Minister Mahmoud Abbas in Aqaba, Jordan, to discuss implementation of a “road-map” for peace.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush, Sharon, Abbas meet

events.title: Federal law enforcement and race
events.description: President Bush issues comprehensive guidelines forbidding federal law enforcement agencies from considering race or ethnicity in routine patrol duties. Although more extensive than previous federal law, the guidelines provided clear exceptions for matters of national security and counterterrorism operations.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Federal law enforcement and race

events.title: Justice Department probes operative leaks
events.description: The Justice Department announces a full criminal investigation into allegations that Bush administration officials had leaked the name of a covert CIA operative to the media in July. Bush urges full cooperation with the probe.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Justice Department probes operative leaks

events.title: Bush bans late term abortions
events.description: Bush signs into law a ban on late-term abortion, the first law to ban an abortion procedure since the Supreme Court’s 1973 decision in Roe v. The Supreme Court later upholds the ban.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush bans late term abortions

events.title: Medicare overhaul
events.description: Bush signs a landmark bill overhauling Medicare that includes the program’s first prescription drug benefits to begin in 2006 and creates incentives for private insurance companies to cover Medicare subscribers.
events.impact: Provided healthcare for elderly Americans

events.title: Bush delivers fourth State of the Union
events.description: Bush gives his fourth State of the Union Address, laying out a broad domestic and foreign policy agenda while stressing issues of national security.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush delivers fourth State of the Union

events.title: CIA Director Tenet resigns
events.description: Bush announces he has accepted the resignation of CIA Director George Tenet, widely blamed for intelligence failures in the months leading up to September 11.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: CIA Director Tenet resigns

events.title: Bush reelected
events.description: Bush wins a second term with a close 51 percent of the popular vote and 274 electoral votes to John Kerry’s 252. The Republican Party builds slightly on its majority in the House and Senate.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush reelected

events.title: Powell Resigns, Rice appointed
events.description: Retired Army general and Secretary of State Colin Powell resigns. Bush appoints former National Security Advisor Condoleezza Rice to the position.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Powell Resigns, Rice appointed

events.title: Bush inaugurated
events.description: President Bush is sworn in for the second term of his presidency.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush inaugurated

events.title: Bush delivers State of the Union
events.description: In his State of the Union, President Bush calls for an historic restructuring of Social Security, allowing workers to use their payroll taxes to invest in the stock market. However, he is unable to move the policy through Congress.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush delivers State of the Union

events.title: Appointing new “Intelligence Czar”
events.description: Bush names former U.S. Ambassador to Iraq John Negroponte Director of National Intelligence, a newly created position of “intelligence czar” created in the wake of Congressional investigations into intelligence failures leading up to September 11.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Appointing new “Intelligence Czar”

events.title: Bush takes diplomatic trip to Europe
events.description: Bush travels to Europe to meet with French President Jacques Chirac, German Chancellor Gerhard Schroeder, and Russian President Vladimir Putin to smooth diplomatic relations after the U.S.-led invasion of Iraq.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush takes diplomatic trip to Europe

events.title: Bush vetoes stem cell research
events.description: President Bush vetoes a bill to lift constraints on federal funding of embryonic stem cell research, and subsequently, the House unsuccessfully attempts to override the veto. This is the first veto Bush issues during his administration.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush vetoes stem cell research

events.title: Bush approves border fence
events.description: President Bush signs a bill providing for the construction of a 700-mile fence along the United States-Mexico border, in an effort to increase border security and stem illegal immigration.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush approves border fence

events.title: Increasing troops in Iraq
events.description: Bush announces what would be termed a “troop surge” in Iraq in an attempt to increase security in the capital of Baghdad and smother insurgency centers throughout the country.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Increasing troops in Iraq

events.title: Bush vetoes troop removal
events.description: Bush vetoes a war spending bill passed by Congress, which set a timetable for troop withdrawal from Iraq. Within days Bush reaches a record low approval rating.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Bush vetoes troop removal

events.title: Gonzalez resigns, Mukasey appointed AG
events.description: President Bush names Michael Mukasey as Attorney General after Alberto Gonzalez announces his resignation.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Gonzalez resigns, Mukasey appointed AG

events.title: Middle East Peace Conference
events.description: President Bush hosts a Middle East Peace Conference in Annapolis, Maryland, with Prime Minister Ehud Olmert of Israel and Palestinian president Mahmoud Abbas.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Middle East Peace Conference

events.title: Increasing automobile fuel efficiency
events.description: Congress passes new energy legislation to increase automobile fuel efficiency standards and mandates increases in biofuel production. The bill passes the House and Senate, and President Bush signs it into law.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Increasing automobile fuel efficiency

events.title: Bush proposes stimulus package
events.description: President Bush proposes a $145 billion stimulus package in response to a housing crisis and rapidly increasing oil prices. The package gives individuals several hundred dollars to facilitate spending, as well as rebates for families with children and tax deductions for businesses in order to jump-start the slowing economy.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush proposes stimulus package

events.title: Bush signs largest bailout in history
events.description: At the onset of financial crisis, President Bush signs a $700 billion bailout plan for failing bank assets, the largest in U.S
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush signs largest bailout in history

events.title: Bush bails out GM, Chrysler
events.description: President Bush issues a $17.4-billion auto bailout to General Motors and Chrysler to keep the two American automotive giants from going bankrupt.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Bush bails out GM, Chrysler

events.title: Barack Obama inaugurated
events.description: Barack Obama is inaugurated the 44th President of the United States. President Bush leaves Washington, D.C. for Dallas, Texas.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Barack Obama inaugurated

events.title: Obama wins election
events.description: Barack Obama is elected president by defeating Senator John McCain with nearly 53 percent of the popular vote and 365 electoral votes (out of 538).
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Obama wins election

events.title: Inauguration
events.description: On January 20, 2009, Barack Obama was inaugurated as the 44th President of the United States. He was the first African American President in U.S. Obama emerged as an unlikely candidate during the 2008 presidential campaign. He was born and raised in Hawaii. His mother, a white American from Kansas, and his father, a black Kenyan studying in the United States, met as students at the University of Hawaii. After attending school in Hawaii, Obama moved to the mainland for college—first attending Occidental College in Los Angeles, California, and then graduating from Columbia University in New York City. Obama then moved to Chicago, Illinois, and began working as a community organizer, helping low-income residents improve their public housing conditions. In 1988, he enrolled at Harvard Law School. During the 1990-1991 academic year, he was president of the prestigious Harvard Law Review, the first African American elected to the position. [truncated]
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Inauguration

events.title: Executive order to close Guantánamo Bay
events.description: Obama signs an executive order to close the U.S. military prison at Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, within one year. Guantánamo Bay is a detention center where individuals considered to be a national threat to the United States are detained and questioned in controversial conditions. His executive order encountered strong Congressional resistance, and the prison remained open after he left office.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Executive order to close Guantánamo Bay

events.title: New age Fireside Chats
events.description: Obama hosts the first-ever online discussion at the White House. He livestreams himself answering questions asked by people online via the administration’s website.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: New age Fireside Chats

events.title: Justice Sotomayor
events.description: Obama nominates federal judge Sonia Sotomayor to take over for retiring Justice David Souter. She is confirmed in August.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Justice Sotomayor

events.title: HIV travel ban
events.description: Obama lifts a 22-year-old ban that restricts those with HIV/AIDS from entering the United States.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: HIV travel ban

events.title: Haiti aid
events.description: Obama pledges $100,000,000 to assist Haiti in recovery from the devastating earthquake that hit the country on January 12.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Haiti aid

events.title: Volcker Rule
events.description: Obama announces the Volcker Rule, which seeks to restrict U.S. banks from engaging in certain speculative investments that would not be in the best interest of the customer.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Volcker Rule

events.title: Nuclear reactor loans
events.description: Obama declares more than $8.3 billion in loan guarantees to Southern Company to construct two new nuclear reactors in Burke County, Georgia.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Nuclear reactor loans

events.title: School grants
events.description: Obama announces $900 million in grants that will be given to under-performing schools if they accept new reforms.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: School grants

events.title: East Coast drilling
events.description: Obama announces he will approve exploration for oil and gas in the Gulf of Mexico and off the coast of Virginia, which had been previously banned.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: East Coast drilling

events.title: Budget funding for NASA
events.description: Obama proposes adding $6 billion to NASA’s budget over the next five years. The funding would be used for space exploration rather than lunar exploration.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Budget funding for NASA

events.title: West Virginia coal miners
events.description: Obama delivers a eulogy for the 29 West Virginia coal miners who died in an explosion earlier in the month.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: West Virginia coal miners

events.title: Justice Kagan
events.description: Obama nominates Solicitor General Elena Kagan to the U.S. Supreme Court. She is sworn into office in August.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Justice Kagan

events.title: Rosa’s law
events.description: Obama signs Rosa’s Law, which changes the diction of federal statutes that use “mental retardation” to “intellectual disability.”
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Rosa’s law

events.title: Two-year pay freeze proposal
events.description: In attempt to get the deficit under control, the president freezes pay raises on federal employees for two years starting on January 1, 2011.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Two-year pay freeze proposal

events.title: First presidential pardons
events.description: President Obama pardons nine individuals with crimes ranging from drug charges to conspiracy.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: First presidential pardons

events.title: Healthy kids
events.description: Obama signs the Healthy, Hunger-Free Kids Act of 2010. This funds nutrition and free lunch programs, sets new standards for schools, and allocates money for the implementation of these new provisions.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Healthy kids

events.title: Tucson shootings
events.description: At a constituent meeting in Tucson, Arizona, a gunman open fired, shooting 19 people, killing 6 of them. Representative Gabrielle Giffords was shot in the head; she resigned her seat in the House to focus on her recovery. The President and First Lady traveled to Tucson, and Obama offered his remarks during a memorial service for the victims.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Tucson shootings

events.title: New START treaty
events.description: Obama signs a nuclear arms treaty with Russia, called New START, which seeks to develop our security, relations with Russia, and a world without the fear of nuclear weapons.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: New START treaty

events.title: Military trials for detainees
events.description: Obama orders the end on the ban on military trials for detainees at Guantánamo Bay. This order permits the United States to resume military trials for detainees
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Military trials for detainees

events.title: Osama Bin Laden killed
events.description: Obama announces the death of Osama Bin Laden, who was the leader of Al-Qaeda, the terrorist group that claimed responsibility for the U.S. terrorist attacks on September 11, 2001. (Bin Laden was killed on May 2, 2011, in Pakistan but it was still May 1 in the United States when the president announced his death.)
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Osama Bin Laden killed

events.title: American Jobs Act
events.description: President Obama makes an address to the joint session of Congress to present the American Jobs Act, which is designed to improve the U.S
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: American Jobs Act

events.title: End of Iraq War
events.description: President Obama announces that U.S. troops will be home from Iraq by December and that the U.S. role in the war is over.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: End of Iraq War

events.title: Iranian sanctions
events.description: Obama signs an executive order that freezes the assets of the Iranian government held within the United States due to Iran engaging in what Obama called, “deceptive practices.”
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Iranian sanctions

events.title: Gay marriage
events.description: President Obama announces his support of same-sex marriage.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Gay marriage

events.title: Democratic National Convention
events.description: Obama accepts the Democratic nomination for president and speaks to the convention.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Democratic National Convention

events.title: Reelected
events.description: Obama defeats Republican Mitt Romney to win a second term in office with 332 electoral votes to 206 and 51 percent of the popular vote.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Reelected

events.title: Sandy Hook shooting
events.description: The president addresses the nation after a shooting at the Sandy Hook elementary school in Connecticut results in the death of 20 children and 6 adults. He pledges to fight for gun control legislation.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Sandy Hook shooting

events.title: Taxpayer relief
events.description: Obama signs the American Taxpayer Relief Act. This extended tax breaks for the majority of Americans while raising the tax rates for top earners.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Taxpayer relief

events.title: Second Inauguration
events.description: Barack Obama is inaugurated into his second term as president. He was sworn into office in a private ceremony on Sunday, January 20, but the official ceremony took place the next day.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Second Inauguration

events.title: Climate plan
events.description: In a speech at Georgetown University, the president announces the Climate Action Plan, which is designed to lower carbon pollution, prepare for the impending effects of climate change, and to address reversing climate change as a global endeavor.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Climate plan

events.title: “Trayvon Martin could have been me”
events.description: Obama delivers remarks on the murder of Trayvon Martin. He discusses why the African American community was enraged at the acquittal of Trayvon Martin’s killer, George Zimmerman. Obama stated, “Trayvon Martin could have been me 35 years ago.”
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: “Trayvon Martin could have been me”

events.title: Student loans
events.description: Obama signs into law the Bipartisan Student Loan Certainty Act of 2013. This act outlines how the government should distribute loans to students and other tactics for helping students with crippling debt.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Student loans

events.title: Climate change and its effects
events.description: Obama signs an executive order in order to prepare the United States for climate change. The order addresses the impacts of climate change and details how the United States will take action to increase readiness against climate change.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Climate change and its effects

events.title: State of the Union
events.description: Obama gives his State of the Union address, focusing on the country’s low unemployment and increasing inequality.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: State of the Union

events.title: Minimum wage
events.description: Obama signs an executive order to increase the minimum wage from $7.25 to $10.10 for all workers on federal contracts.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Minimum wage

events.title: Russian sanctions
events.description: Obama calls for economic sanctions against those responsible for the Russian intervention in Crimea and the stealing of assets of the Ukrainian people.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Russian sanctions

events.title: LGBT discrimination
events.description: Obama signs an executive order to protect the rights of LGBT employees in the workforce. This does not include an exemption for religious beliefs, which means people cannot discriminate based on their religious preference.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: LGBT discrimination

events.title: Immigration reform
events.description: Obama makes speech to the nation on comprehensive immigration reform. He announces he will use his executive power to allow illegal immigrants currently living within the United States to legally stay and obtain work.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Immigration reform

events.title: Free community college
events.description: Obama proposes to make community college free for students if they adhere to certain criteria such as maintaining a certain GPA. He also outlines what community colleges and the federal government must do in order to facilitate this change.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Free community college

events.title: Eulogy for Reverend Clementa Pinckney
events.description: Obama delivers a eulogy for Reverend Clementa Pinckney in Charleston, South Carolina, following a racially charged shooting that killed nine people worshipping inside a church.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Eulogy for Reverend Clementa Pinckney

events.title: Pope Francis
events.description: Obama welcomes Pope Francis for visit at the White House.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Pope Francis

events.title: Deal with Iran
events.description: Obama announces historic agreement with Iran to prevent the acquisition of nuclear weapons. Iran met conditions for the United States to begin lifting some economic sanctions.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Deal with Iran

events.title: Death of Antonin Scalia
events.description: Supreme Court Justice Antonin Scalia is found dead. Obama nominated Merrick Garland to replace Scalia on the Supreme Court but Republicans in Congress will not hold confirmation hearings.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Death of Antonin Scalia

events.title: Flint, Michigan visit
events.description: Following the discovery that Flint, Michigan’s water supply had been insufficiently treated and had exposed its residents to dangerously high levels of lead, the President traveled to Flint to try to reassure the people.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Flint, Michigan visit

events.title: Gives speech at Democratic National Convention
events.description: Obama gives a speech at the Democratic National Convention in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, in support of Democratic presidential candidate Hillary Clinton. November 8, 2016: Presidential election Republican candidate Donald J. Trump defeats Hillary Clinton, winning the presidency with 306 electoral votes (although he lost the popular vote by nearly 3 million votes). November 10, 2016: President-elect visits White House President Obama hosts President-elect Donald Trump at the White House, meeting with the press in the Oval Office. January 20, 2017: Leaving office President Obama concludes his last day in office. After attending the inauguration of President Trump, the Obamas head off for a vacation in Palm Springs, California. The Obama announced that they will continue to live in Washington, DC, until their younger daughter finishes high school.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Gives speech at Democratic National Convention

events.title: Trump inaugurated
events.description: Trump is inaugurated as the 45th President of the United States.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump inaugurated

events.title: Withdrawal from the Trans-Pacific Partnership
events.description: President Trump directs the office of the US trade representatives to withdraw the United States from the Trans-Pacific Partnership.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Withdrawal from the Trans-Pacific Partnership

events.title: Global gag rule reinstated
events.description: In one of his first policy decisions, President Trump decides to reinstate the “Mexico City Policy,” a ban on aid to international groups performing or counseling on abortion. President Ronald Reagan first introduced this ban in 1984 at a U.N conference in Mexico City, and U.S. presidents have consistently rescinded and reinstated since.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Global gag rule reinstated

events.title: Travel ban
events.description: President Trump signs an executive order that denies entry into the United States for people from Iran, Iraq, Libya, Somalia, Sudan, Syria, and Yemen, and suspends the Refugee Admissions Policy for 120 days. Trump’s travel ban sparks large protests and legal challenges. As a result of legal challenges, Trump revises the executive order.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Travel ban

events.title: Gorsuch nominated for SCOTUS
events.description: President Trump announces his nomination of Neil Gorsuch to the US Supreme Court. Judge Gorsuch will fill the seat vacated when Justice Antonin Scalia died in February 2016. The US Senate denied President Barack Obama the chance to fill the seat.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Gorsuch nominated for SCOTUS

events.title: New executive order on the travel ban
events.description: President Trump signs a revised travel ban to respond to legal challenges to the first travel ban. The new travel ban implements a travel ban from six Muslim majority countries and temporarily suspends the Refugee Admission Program.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: New executive order on the travel ban

events.title: Reversal on environmental protection
events.description: President Trump signs an executive order that rolls back the Obama administration’s temporary ban on mining coal and a protection rule for streams. Trump reverses much of Obama’s clean power strategy, rolling back US action to combat climate change.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Reversal on environmental protection

events.title: Action against Syrian government
events.description: President Trump orders US strikes against an air base in Syria after the Syrian government launches a chemical weapons attack against the Syrian province of Idlib, killing civilians including children.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Action against Syrian government

events.title: FBI director James Comey removed
events.description: President Donald Trump fires director of the FBI, James Comey. The firing is unexpected and raises concerns about abuse of power and the independence of the FBI.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: FBI director James Comey removed

events.title: Withdrawal from Paris climate accord
events.description: President Trump announces that the United States will withdraw from the 2015 Paris climate accord, another agreement negotiated by Obama.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Withdrawal from Paris climate accord

events.title: Chief of Staff Kelly named
events.description: President Trump announces that he has replaced Reince Priebus as his chief of staff with John F. Kelly, former general and secretary of Homeland Security.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Chief of Staff Kelly named

events.title: US increases presence in Afghanistan
events.description: President Trump announces a new US strategy in Afghanistan, increasing the number of US troops in the country to try to achieve a stable peace.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: US increases presence in Afghanistan

events.title: UN Address
events.description: President Trump addresses the 72nd session of the United Nations General Assembly in New York City. He focuses on his message of "America First" and discusses the ongoing situations with North Korea, Iran, and Syria.
events.impact: Created framework for international cooperation

events.title: Trump officials plead in court
events.description: Trump’s former campaign chairman Paul Manafort pleads not guilty on charges of laundering money. George Papadopoulos, a former foreign policy adviser to the Trump campaign, pleads guilty to lying to the FBI and is cooperating with Mueller investigation.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump officials plead in court

events.title: Tax bill signed
events.description: President Trump signs $1.5 trillion tax bill into law. The bill overhauls the federal tax code and is a victory for Republicans. The bill cuts taxes for corporations and the wealthy while delivering only moderate cuts to most Americans.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Tax bill signed

events.title: Trade War with China Begins
events.description: Prrsident Trump launches a still-running tariff war against China by slapping tariffs of between 30 and 50 per cent on solar panels and washing machines. Tariffs are gradually applied to other US imports from China and to other countries.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trade War with China Begins

events.title: New secretary of state
events.description: Secretary of State Rex Tillerson serves his last day in office. President Trump nominates CIA Director Mike Pompeo to replace him.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: New secretary of state

events.title: Withdrawal from Iran nuclear deal
events.description: President Trump announces that he is pulling the United States out of the deal with Iran to contain its nuclear program.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Withdrawal from Iran nuclear deal

events.title: Trump meets with North Korean leader
events.description: President Trump meets with North Korean leader, Kim Jong-un, at a summit in Singapore.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump meets with North Korean leader

events.title: Controversial child separation at the border
events.description: President Trumps signs an executive order ending the policy of separating children from their parents. Between April and June, almost 2,000 children were separated from their parents as they crossed the border into the United States. After public opinion widely condemned the separations, the Trump administration ended the policy.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Controversial child separation at the border

events.title: Kavanaugh nominated for SCOTUS
events.description: President Trump nominates Brett Kavanaugh to the US Supreme Court after Justice Anthony Kennedy announces his resignation.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Kavanaugh nominated for SCOTUS

events.title: Trump meets with Putin
events.description: President Trump and President Vladimir Putin of Russia meet in Helsinki, Finland, for a summit. During the press conference, Trump refuses to speak out against Russian involvement in the 2016 US elections, and he receives criticism from Democrats and Republicans for not standing up to Russia.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump meets with Putin

events.title: Attorney General resigns
events.description: The Attorney General of the United States, Jeff Sessions, resigns from office after President Trump requests his resignation.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Attorney General resigns

events.title: Secretary of Defense resigns
events.description: Secretary of Defense James Mattis resigns after President Trump announces he is pulling US troops out of Syria, a decision that Mattis opposes. Trump appoints Patrick Shanahan as acting defense secretary.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Secretary of Defense resigns

events.title: Trump announces reopening plan
events.description: After the longest government shutdown in US history, 35 days, President Trump announces an agreement to reopen the government. Although the President had demanded funding for a wall along the southern border, he agrees to reopen the government while negotiations continue over how to fund the building of the wall and border security.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump announces reopening plan

events.title: Border wall funding becomes national emergency
events.description: President Trump declares a national emergency to secure funds to build a wall at the southern border.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Border wall funding becomes national emergency

events.title: Mueller report released
events.description: Special Prosecutor Robert Mueller releases his report on the Russian interference in the 2016 presidential election and the Trump campaign’s involvement to the Justice Department.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Mueller report released

events.title: Reelection campaign launched
events.description: At a rally in Florida, President Trump officially launches his 2020 reelection campaign.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Reelection campaign launched

events.title: Trump call to Ukraine president
events.description: President Trump speaks to President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine. In the call, President Trump pushes Zelensky to investigate alleged corruption by Democratic presidential candidate Joe Biden and his son Hunter.
events.impact: Government corruption

events.title: Space Command
events.description: President Trump announces the creation of the US Space Command, which will be responsible for military operations in space as part of the Department of Defense.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Space Command

events.title: White House Coronavirus Task Force created
events.description: As the coronavirus, which causes Covid-19, spreads worldwide, President Trump appoints Vice President Mike Pence to oversee the management of the virus. Pence works with doctors and health officials to coordinate a response to the virus. By March 1, the virus has killed two people in Washington state and has appeared in a handful of other states, including California, Florida, Illinois, New York, Oregon, and Rhode Island.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: White House Coronavirus Task Force created

events.title: Coronavirus pandemic escalates
events.description: The World Health Organization announces that the coronavirus has become a global pandemic, and President Trump addresses the American public to announce a 30-day travel ban on Europeans coming into the United States. Meanwhile daily life in the United States is disrupted as schools, colleges, and universities move to online classes, events are canceled, and people are encouraged to limit their interactions with others.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Coronavirus pandemic escalates

events.title: Pandemic becomes national emergency
events.description: President Trump declares a national emergency in response to the coronavirus. As Americans are urged to stay home and create social distance with others to slow the virus, Trump announces the national emergency to free up funds and allow government agencies more latitude in protecting people. He also asks every hospital in the country to activate its emergency preparedness plan so that they can meet the needs of Americans everywhere.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Pandemic becomes national emergency

events.title: Economic shutdown and relief efforts
events.description: The United States leads the globe with the most cases of coronavirus. As the virus spreads throughout the country, more states and cities try to limit people’s activities, shutting down business, restaurants, bars, and events. President Trump signs a $2-trillion emergency spending bill, the Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security (CARES) Act, which provides money for individuals, corporations and small businesses, and state and local governments.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Economic shutdown and relief efforts

events.title: Withdrawal from the international arms treaty
events.description: President Donald Trump announces at the National Rifle Association's annual meeting that the United States will drop out of an international arms treaty, which then-President Barack Obama signed in 2013 but the NRA and other conservative groups oppose.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Withdrawal from the international arms treaty

events.title: Social media national security concerns
events.description: President Trump signs executive orders banning the use of TikTok and WeChat in the United States within 45 days if their Chinese parent companies refuse to sell them as a result of national security concerns.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Social media national security concerns

events.title: Trump expands economic relief
events.description: President Trumps signs four executive orders to help the American people during the coronavirus pandemic after negotiations with Democrats in Congress stall. The four orders include a payroll tax holiday, extending unemployment benefits, providing protections to prevent evictions, and suspending student loan payments.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump expands economic relief

events.title: Trump swiftly announces Supreme Court nomination
events.description: President Trump nominates Judge Amy Coney Barret, a conservative Federal Appeals Court Judge, to succeed the late Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg on the US Supreme Court.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump swiftly announces Supreme Court nomination

events.title: Trump's final campaign rallies
events.description: In the last few days of his campaign before the election, Donald Trump hosts several large campaign rallies in key swing states, gathering large crowds of supporters
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump's final campaign rallies

events.title: Trump declares victory
events.description: In the early morning hours, President Trump declares victory in the 2020 presidential election and claims the remaining ballots should be cast out due to fraud.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump declares victory

events.title: Trump campaign contests election
events.description: Directly after the election results are announced, President Trump and his campaign began filing lawsuits across key swing states in an attempt to contest the election by claiming mass amounts of voter fraud from mail-in ballots.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump campaign contests election

events.title: Electoral College declares the 46th POTUS
events.description: The Electoral College officially votes Joe Biden as president and Senator Kamala Harris as vice president of the United States. This decision ends the legal battle initiated by the Trump campaign which alleged mass amounts of voter fraud in the election.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Electoral College declares the 46th POTUS

events.title: Phone call leaked
events.description: President Trump calls the Secretary of State of Georgia to pressure him to "find" votes so that Trump can win Georgia. The Secretary tapes the conversation and leaks it to the press the next day.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Phone call leaked

events.title: Mob attacks US Capitol
events.description: Trump supporters and white supremacists gather to protest the results of the 2020 presidential election. A mob overwhelms Capitol police and pushes their way into the US Capitol, where Congress is meeting to certify the election results. As members of Congress rush out of harm's way, police are unable to contain the intruders who broke windows, destroyed property, and trespassed through the building. The police shoot and kill one pro-Trump demonstrator as she tries to break through a door, and one Capitol police officer dies of injuries sustained during the riot.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: Mob attacks US Capitol

events.title: Flurry of executive orders
events.description: In his first few days in office, President Joe Biden signs numerous executive orders, including rejoining the World Health Organization and the Paris Climate Accord, allowing transgendered people to serve in the military, and creating the Covid-19 Health Equity Task Force.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Flurry of executive orders

events.title: American Rescue Plan
events.description: On the anniversary of the Covid-19 shutdown, President Biden signs the American Rescue Plan Act, a $1.9-trillion economic stimulus bill to combat the effects of the Covid-19 pandemic.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: American Rescue Plan

events.title: The American Jobs Plan
events.description: In a speech in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, President Biden announces the details of the American Jobs Plan, which will modernize the country's infrastructure, combat the effects of climate change, and help middle-class workers.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: The American Jobs Plan

events.title: Juneteenth holiday
events.description: Joe Biden signs a bill creating a federal holiday for Juneteenth, June 19th, to commemorate the end of slavery in the United States.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Juneteenth holiday

events.title: Biden signs infrastructure bill
events.description: President Biden signs the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act, a $1.2 trillion bill to improve roads, bridges, public transit, as well as increased access to clean water and the internet.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Biden signs infrastructure bill

events.title: Anniversary of January 6
events.description: On the one year anniversary of the January 6 Capitol riot, President Biden addresses the American people from the US Capitol.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Anniversary of January 6

events.title: Sanctions against Russia
events.description: After President Vladimir Putin of Russia sends troops into two separatist-held regions of Ukraine, President Biden announces US sanctions against Russia. The sanctions target Russian finances and banking.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Sanctions against Russia

events.title: Supreme Court nomination
events.description: President Biden nominates Judge Ketanji Brown Jackson to the US Supreme Court.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Supreme Court nomination

events.title: Ban on Russian oil and gas
events.description: President Biden announces that the United States will ban imports of natural gas and oil from Russia in an attempt to put pressure on President Putin to stop the war in Ukraine.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Ban on Russian oil and gas

events.title: Travel to Europe
events.description: President Biden travels to Europe in support of Ukraine. He attends an EU Summit meeting and a NATO meeting and gives a speech in Poland.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Travel to Europe

events.title: Antilynching legislation
events.description: President Biden signs the Emmett Till Antilynching Act, making lynching a federal crime.
events.impact: Racial terrorism and murder

events.title: Safer Communities Act
events.description: President Biden signs the Safer Communities Act, which addresses issues related to gun safety, school safety, and mental health to try to reduce gun violence. This bill is the first major federal gun control law in decades.
events.impact: Moderately positive impact: Safer Communities Act

events.title: CHIPS and Science Act
events.description: On August 9, 2022, President Joe Biden signed the CHIPS and Science Act, which appropriated $280 billion for semiconductor and chip research and manufacturing.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: CHIPS and Science Act

events.title: Inflation Reduction Act
events.description: President Biden signs the Inflation Reduction Act, which lowers health care costs and focuses on tackling the climate crisis by reducing pollution and investing in clean energy.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Inflation Reduction Act

events.title: Marijuana pardons
events.description: President Biden pardons all people convicted of marijuana possession under federal law and asks for a review of whether marijuana should be in the same legal category as heroin and LSD.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Marijuana pardons

events.title: Same-Sex Marriage Bill
events.description: Biden signs the Respect for Marriage Act into law. The bill provides federal protections for same-sex and interracial marriages.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Same-Sex Marriage Bill

events.title: Electoral reform
events.description: President Biden signs the Electoral Count Reform and Presidential Transition Improvement Act of 2022, election reforms designed to prevent another January 6 attack. Along with other provisions, it outlines processes for how to handle disputes over counting electoral votes and the role of the vice president.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Electoral reform

events.title: US tanks to Urkraine
events.description: President Biden announces that the United States will send 31 battle tanks to Ukraine to help them defeat the Russian invasion.
events.impact: Moderately negative impact: US tanks to Urkraine

events.title: Chinese balloon shot down
events.description: President Biden orders military aircraft to shoot down a high-altitude balloon from China that has flown over the country.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Chinese balloon shot down

events.title: First veto
events.description: President Biden issues the first veto of his presidency. He vetoes a bill that would ban the government from considering environmental impacts when making investments for retirement plans.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: First veto

events.title: Reelection campaign
events.description: President Biden announces his campaign for reelection for a second term as president.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Reelection campaign

events.title: Trip to Maui after wildfire devestation
events.description: President and First Lady Biden travel to Maui, Hawaii, to tour the devestation from a wildfire on the island. A few hundred people died after the fires spread through the town of Lahaina.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trip to Maui after wildfire devestation

events.title: Office of Gun Violence Prevention
events.description: President Biden announces the creation of the White House Office of Gun Violence Prevention.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Office of Gun Violence Prevention

events.title: Biden visits striking workers
events.description: President Biden visits members of the United Auto Workers union who are striking to show his support for organized labor. It is the first time a modern president has joined a picket line.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Biden visits striking workers

events.title: Biden travels to Israel
events.description: President Biden travels to Israel to signal US support for the Israelis after the Hamas attack.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Biden travels to Israel

events.title: Executive order on artificial intelligence
events.description: President Biden signs an executive order on artificial intelligence that attempts to protect Americans from the risks of AI.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Executive order on artificial intelligence

events.title: Aid to Ukraine, Israel, and Taiwan
events.description: President Joe Biden signs a $95.3 billion package of aid to Ukraine, Israel, and Taiwan, after months of debate in Congress led to a delay in approving the package.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Aid to Ukraine, Israel, and Taiwan

events.title: Executive order to limit migrants
events.description: President Biden issues an executive order limiting asylum seekers at the US border. The order blocks migrants from crossing into the United States seeking asylum if the number of asylum seekers is more than 2,500 per day.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Executive order to limit migrants

events.title: Hunter Biden found guilty
events.description: President Biden's son Hunter is found guilty on three counts of lying on a federal application for a firearm.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Hunter Biden found guilty

events.title: Withdrawing from the campaign
events.description: In a letter posted on social media, President Biden announces that he will not seek reelection and will withdraw from the 2024 presidential campaign. He endorses Vice President Kamala Harris.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Withdrawing from the campaign

events.title: Prisoner swap
events.description: President Biden announces a prisoner swap with Russia, resulting in the return of 16 people in exchange for 8 Russians.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Prisoner swap

events.title: Trump visits White House
events.description: President Biden hosts President-elect Trump at the White House to discuss the transition of power.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Trump visits White House

events.title: Pardons son
events.description: President Biden pardons his son, Hunter Biden, from federal convictions related to tax evasion and illegally purchasing a gun.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Pardons son

events.title: Preemptive pardons
events.description: On his last day in office, President Joe Biden issues preemptive pardons to Dr. Anthony Fauci, General Mark Milley, and members of the January 6 congressional committee to protect them from possible prosecution by the incoming Trump administration.
events.impact: Mixed or neutral impact: Preemptive pardons
